definition: Represent each sample by batch, plate, well position, cell type, assay protocol, reagent lot, instrument, and acquisition date when available. Compute metadata similarity or domain-level distribution similarity to audit whether performance depends on technical execution overlap rather than biological signal.
example_usage: python metadata_distance_similarity.py --train train_assays.csv --eval eval_assays.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --columns batch_id plate_id cell_type protocol instrument

citations.title: RxRx1: A Dataset for Evaluating Experimental Batch Correction Methods

evidence.summary: RxRx1 is designed to evaluate batch correction across microscopy images from many experimental batches and cell types.
The paper records plate, imaging, perturbation, cell type, and batch structure and defines batch-separated vs batch-stratified splits.
The benchmark measures perturbation accuracy on unseen batches and whether embeddings remain batch-invariant.

limitations: Metadata may be incomplete and does not capture all latent technical variation.
Batch, cell type, treatment, and date can be confounded.
Exact metadata matching can be too brittle when protocol variables are continuous.
quality_gates: Report all batch/protocol fields and known confounders.
Compare metadata similarity with feature-distribution similarity across batches.
Audit whether biological labels are balanced across technical domains.
